creator: yuri@freebsd.org
text:
(In reply to Adam Weinberger from comment #3)

> Does this benefit anything other than "svn log"?

This is an easy way to improve the readability of "svn log". Single port commits must be the majority of commits.

> When 25 ports have a "Update to version 2.2.33" commit message, you have no idea which port was actually updated when viewing the log for the other 24 ports.

Maybe the same should be done for multi-port commits too? In case of a multi-port commit, the subversion hook script could automatically add the list of ports to the end of the message?
